reasoning: The Lean statement takes arbitrary groups G, H, K and two group homomorphisms f : G →* H and g : H →* K, assumes each is surjective as a function, and concludes that g.comp f is surjective. Since MonoidHom.comp (used via g.comp f) automatically produces a group homomorphism G →* K, asserting Function.Surjective on it is equivalent to asserting that the composition "is a surjective homomorphism" as in the informal statement. Hypotheses and conclusion correspond exactly to the informal claim, with no extra or missing assumptions. Hence the formalization is strictly faithful.
